findings.issue: InvitationPage.test.tsx initOAuth failure test ("shows error when initOAuth fails") does not assert sessionStorage cleanup. The production code in InvitationPage.tsx:59-60 calls clearVerifier() and clearInvitationToken() in the catch block, and the test populates sessionStorage via storeVerifier/StoreInvitationToken before initOAuth rejects, but the test never checks that oauth_verifier and invitation_token are null after the error. Every other error-path test in InvitationCompletePage.test.tsx and AppRouter.test.tsx asserts sessionStorage cleanup.

In InvitationPage.tsx handleOAuthLogin, a new AbortController is created on line 41 and its signal is passed to initOAuth on line 53, but the controller is never aborted anywhere. The guard `if (abortController.signal.aborted) return` on line 56 is dead code. Unlike the useEffect patterns in OAuthCallbackPage and InvitationCompletePage where the AbortController is aborted in the cleanup function, this handler has no mechanism to abort the controller. The loading state and disabled buttons already prevent concurrent calls.
findings.remediation: Remove the unused AbortController and the dead abort check, or add an AbortController that can be aborted on component unmount (e.g., ref stored in a useEffect cleanup).
